name: Kindergarten Tabletop Game Design — Semester 2
description: An 18-week, play-based Kindergarten pathway for making, testing, and improving a simple cooperative game. Lessons are brief, hands-on, flexible, and designed for adult-guided learning.
learningGoals: Build confidence and curiosity with making, testing, and improving a simple cooperative game.
Communicate learning through talk, drawing, movement, play, or a simple creation.
Practice safe, kind, and responsible participation.

lessons.title: Notice and talk: making, testing, and improving a simple cooperative game
lessons.objective: I can notice details and share a question about making, testing, and improving a simple cooperative game through a close look at making, testing, and improving a simple cooperative game.

lessons.objective: I can notice details and share a question about making, testing, and improving a simple cooperative game through a guided practice with making, testing, and improving a simple cooperative game.

lessons.objective: I can notice details and share a question about making, testing, and improving a simple cooperative game through a small creation or demonstration about making, testing, and improving a simple cooperative game.

activities.title: A close look at making, testing, and improving a simple cooperative game
activities.learnerAction: With an adult or teacher nearby when needed, the learner participates in a close look at making, testing, and improving a simple cooperative game, uses words, pictures, movement, or materials to communicate thinking, and cleans up safely.

successCriteria: The learner participates safely and respectfully in making, testing, and improving a simple cooperative game.
The learner communicates one observation, choice, or question using speech, drawing, movement, or a supported response.
